content: Self-Refine’s task-focused iterative output polishing and Reflexion’s long-horizon agent memory-driven self-reflection can be combined to create a two-layer autonomous self-improvement system: Self-Refine handles immediate local refinement of individual outputs, while Reflexion accumulates global patterns of past mistakes to guide what Self-Refine should prioritize in each iteration. This creates a hybrid system that retains Self-Refine’s advantage of no extra training while fixing its blind spot of unlearned long-term failure patterns.
